sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, PWK, AP90, GRA, FRI, and BEN source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.note: No stable machine definition could be extracted; source record is preserved in cit/quote.

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote: <L>192832.1<pc>949,3<k1>vi<k2>vi/<e>1A ¦ sometimes it gives a meaning opposite to the idea contained in the simple root (<ab>e.g.</ab> √ <s>krI</s>, ‘to buy’; <s>vi-√ krI</s>, ‘to sell’), or it intensifies that idea (<ab>e.g.</ab> √ <s>hiMs</s>, ‘to injure’; <s>vi-√ hiMs</s>, ‘to injure severely’). The above <hom>3.</hom> <s>vi/</s> may also be used in forming compounds not immediately referable to verbs, in which cases it may express ‘difference’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <hom>1.</hom> <s>vi-lakzaRa</s>), ‘change’ or ‘variety’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-citra</s>), ‘intensity’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-karAla</s>), ‘manifoldness’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-viDa</s>), ‘contrariety’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-loma</s>), ‘deviation from right’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-SIla</s>), ‘negation’ or ‘privation’ (<ab>cf.</ab> <s>vi-kacCa</s>, being often used like <hom>3.</hom> <s>a</s>, <s>nir</s>, and <s>nis</s> [<ab>qq.vv.</ab>], and like the <lang>Lat.</lang> <etym>dis</etym>, <etym>se</etym>, and the <lang>English</lang> <etym>a</etym>, <etym>dis</etym>, <etym>in</etym>, <etym>un</etym> &c.)<info lex="inh"/> <LEND>

<L>90743<pc>6-0978<k1>vi<k2>vi<h>1
1. {#vi#}¦ <lex>m.</lex>
<ls>UṆĀDIS. 4,133.</ls> <ab>nom.</ab> <ab>sg.</ab> {#vis#} und {#ves#} (
<ls>ṚV. 6,3,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">1,173,1.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">3,54,6.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">9,72,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,33,2</ls>), <ab>acc.</ab> {#vim#}, <ab>gen.</ab> <ab>abl.</ab> {#ves#}; <ab>nom.</ab> und <ab>acc.</ab> <ab>pl.</ab> {#va/yas#} (
<ls>ṚV. 1,104,1</ls>), {#vi/Bis, vi/Byas, vInA/m#} . {%Vogel%}
<ls>NIR. 2,6.</ls>
<ls>AK. 2,5,33.</ls>
<ls>H. 1316.</ls>
<ls>HALĀY. 2,83.</ls> {#mA mADi^ pu\tre vimi^va graBIzwa#}
<ls>ṚV. 2,29,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 2,">38,7.</ls> {#utte\ vaya^Scidvasa\tera^paptan#}
<ls n="ṚV.">6,64,6.</ls> {#yadvA\M raTo\ viBi\zpatA^t#} der <is>Aśvin</is>
<ls n="ṚV.">1,46,3.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">8,29,8.</ls> {#vayo 'SvA^H#} {%fliegende Rosse%}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,104,1.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 1,">117,14.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">6,63,7.</ls> {#vI\nAM pa\dam#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,25,7.</ls> {#pa\daM veH#}
<ls n="ṚV. 1,">164,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">3,5,5.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 3,5,">6.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 3,">7,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">4,5,8.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">10,5,1.</ls> {#mA\tA pu\trEradi^ti\rDAya^se\ veH#}
<ls n="ṚV.">1,72,9.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">6,48,17.</ls> {#pra su za viByo\ vira^stu#}
<ls n="ṚV.">4,26,4.</ls> {#parRin#}
<ls n="ṚV.">8,5,32.</ls> {#druzad#}
<ls n="ṚV.">9,72,5.</ls> <ls>PAÑCAV. BR. 5,6,15.</ls> {#vaya^H puruzAda^H#} {%die Pfeile%}
<ls>ṚV. 10,27,22.</ls> {#anu^ tvA\ patnI^rhfzi\taM vaya^Sca\ viSve^ de\vAso^ amada\nnanu^ tvA#} nach dem <ab>Comm.</ab> {%die fliegenden%} <is>Marut</is>
<ls n="ṚV.">1,103,7.</ls> <ls n="ṚV.">5,53,3.</ls> <ls>VS. 2,16.</ls> {#catuzpAdvO#}
<ls>VOP. 23,6.</ls> {#nadeByA 'pi hradeByo 'pi pivantyanye vayaH payaH#}
<ls>Spr. 1398.</ls> {#vIn#}
<ls>BHAṬṬ. 9,24.</ls> {#BUtavigrahAH#}
<ls>Spr. 3154.</ls> {#bahuvi jayati vanam#}
<ls>UJJVAL.</ls> zu <ls>UṆĀDIS. 4,133.</ls>
<div n="v">— <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#rAjavi#} und 1. {#vayas#} .
<LEND>

<L>101558<pc>6-075-c<k1>vi<k2>vi/<h>2 <hom>2.</hom> {#vi/#}¦ <lex>Adv.</lex> in Verbindung mit Zeitwörtern und in <ab>Comp.</ab> mit einem Nomen {%Trennung und Abstand%} bezeichnend. Als <ab>Präp.</ab> mit <ab>Acc.</ab> {%hindurch, dazwischen%} (mit Ergänzung eines Verbum <ab>fin.</ab>). Prädicativ so <ab>v. a.</ab> {%auseinanderstehend%} <ls>TBR. 1,6,4,3</ls>. <LEND>

<L>25594<pc>0964-c<k1>vi<k2>vi {#vi#}¦ {%<ab>ind.</ab>%} {@1@} As a prefix to verbs and nouns it expresses:--({%a%}) <lbinfo n="se+paration"/> separation, disjunction (apart, <lbinfo n="asund+er"/> asunder, away, off &c.), as {#viyuj, vihf#}, <lbinfo n="vi#cal"/> {#vical#} &c.; ({%b%}) the reverse of an action; as {#krI#} ‘to buy’; {#vikrI#}, ‘to sell’; {#smf#} ‘to remember’; {#vismf#} ‘to forget’; ({%c%}) division; as {#viBaj#}, {#viBAga#}; ({%d%}) distinction; as <lbinfo n="vi#Siz"/> {#viSiz, viSeza, vivic, viveka#}; ({%e%}) discrimination; {#vyavacCeda#}; ({%f%}) order, arrangement; as {#viDA, virac#}; ({%g%}) opposition; as {#viruD, viroDa#}; privation; as {#vitI, vinayata#}; ({%i%}) deliberation, as {#vicara, vicAra#}; ({%j%}) {{ntensity; {#viDvaMsa#}->intensity; {#viDvaMsa#}||20210104|Dr. Sampada Savardekar and Jim Funderburk|https://github.com/sanskrit-lexicon/csl-corrections/issues/20|}}. {@--2@} As a prefix to nouns or adjectives not <lbinfo n="immedi+ately"/> immediately connected with roots {#vi#} <lbinfo n="ex+presses"/> expresses ({%a%}) negation or privation, in which case it is used much in the same way as {#a#} or {#nir#}, {%<ab>i. e.</ab>%} it forms <ab>Bah.</ab> <ab>comp.</ab>, {#viDavA, vyasuH#} &c.; ({%b%}) intensity, greatness; as {#vikarAla#}; ({%c%}) variety, as {#vicitra#}; ({%d%}) difference; as {#vilakzaRa#}; ({%e%}) manifoldness, as {#viviDa#}; ({%f%}) contrariety, opposition, as {#viloma#}; ({%g%}) change, as {#vikAra#}; ({%h%}) impropriety, as {#vijanman#}. <LEND>

<L>7952.1<pc>1264<k1>vi<k2>vi/<h>1 <hom>1.</hom> {@ví,@}¦ nur als Richtungswort: {%auseinander, hinweg, hindurch%} <ab>u. s. w.</ab>, bei Verben des Tönens [Page1265] oder Leuchtens die Ausbreitung oder Entwickelung des Schalles oder Lichtes bezeichnend, und zwar zusammengefügt mit den Verben: ac, aj, añj, ad, ard, arṣ, aś, <hom>2.</hom> as, āp, i, inv, īr, u, ud, <hom>2.</hom> ūh, karṣ, kas, kāś, <hom>2.</hom> kir, kṛ, <hom>1.</hom> kṛt, kram, krī, kṣar, <hom>2.</hom> kṣi, khād, khyā, gāh, <hom>1.</hom> gir, grabh, <hom>1.</hom> ghar, cakṣ, cat, car, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> ci, cit, cṛt, cyu, jan, ji, jeh, jñā, taṃs, takṣ, <hom>1.</hom> tan, tap, tar, tud, day, dar, das, dah, daś, dus, duh, dṛś, dyut, dru, dham, <hom>1.</hom> dhā, dhāv, dhī, dhū, dhṛ, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> naś, nikṣ, nī, <hom>2.</hom> nu, nud, <hom>1.</hom> pat, pan, <hom>2.</hom> pā, pṛc, pṛch, prath, prā, pru, bandh, bādh, budh, brū, bhaj, bhā, bhid, bhī, bhū, bhūs, bhṛ, bhrāj, maṃh, <hom>1.</hom> man, <hom>1.</hom> mā, mi, muc, mṛj, mṛdh, mṛś, mrad, yam, yā, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> yu, rad, rapś, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>2.</hom> rāj, ri, ric, ruc, ruj, <hom>2.</hom> rudh, ruh, vac, vadh, varh, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>3.</hom> vas, vah, <hom>1.</hom> <hom>3.</hom> vā, vic, vid, viś, <hom>1.</hom> vṛ, vṛt, vṛdh, ven, vraśc, śaṃs, śak, śar, śrath, śri, <hom>1.</hom> śru, śvi, śvit, saj, sapary, sah, <hom>2.</hom> sā, si, su, sṛ, sṛj, sṛp, skabh, skabhāy, stabh, stir, sthā, spac, spṛdh, sphur, sras, han, hary, hā, hu, hū, hṛ, hvṛ; die Zusammensetzung mit Nomen siehe in den folgenden, sofern vi- abgetrennt ist. Wo es scheinbar adverbial steht, ist ein Verb zu ergänzen, zu dem es als Richtungswort gehört, <ab>z. B.</ab> {525,2} ví dúras paṇīnā́m, wo vár zu ergänzen, vielleicht sogar in den <ls>Text</ls> zu fügen ist (<ls>Ku. Zeitschr. 〔16,185〕</ls>); {181,5} (hárī) mathnā́ rájāṃsi aśvinā ví ghóṣais, wo ví dem Sinne nach zu der in mathnā́ [<ab>du.</ab>] enthaltenen Wurzel math gehört (math mit ví zerreissen), also „die beiden Rosse, die Lüfte durchdringend mit Getöse“; in {912,20} káti svid tā́ ví yójanā ist, wenn die Lesart richtig ist, etwa tiṣṭhanti zu ergänzen. <LEND>

<L>6249<pc>267<k1>vi<k2>vi I vi adv. <div n="1"/>1 <lang n="czech">pryč, různě</lang> <div n="1"/>2 <lang n="russian">прочь, различным способом</lang> <div n="1"/>3 <lang n="english">away, asunder, in different directions</lang> <LEND>

<L>13139<pc>0843-b<k1>vi<k2>vi
2. {#vi#}¦ {%vi,%} <ab>m.</ab>, <ab>f.</ab> {%vī̆,%} {@1.@} A bird, <ls>Chr.</ls>
287, 6 = <ls>Rigv.</ls> i. 48, 6. {@2.@} The eye. {@3.@}
Heaven. {@4.@} The region of the wind.
{@ -- <ab>Comp.</ab>@} {%Bahu-,%} <ab>adj.</ab> containing many
birds. -- <ab>Cf.</ab> <ab>Lat.</ab> avis; <lang n="greek">αἰετός, οἰωνίς, ὤϊον, ὠόν</lang>;
<ab>Lat.</ab> ovum; <ab>O.H.G.</ab> ei;
<ab>A.S.</ab> aeg.
<LEND>
